crime.value: Political conspiracy/treason involving exiled nobles and police surveillance
crime.quote: we might have seized your ci-devants any day for the last week; but we knew they were reinstated

closed_world.value: The commune of Cinq-Cygne and surrounding baronial estate under police surveillance
closed_world.quote: ordered to remain in the commune of Cinq-Cygne during the said two years

culprit_and_motive.value: Police agents Corentin and Peyrade tracking political exiles for state security
culprit_and_motive.quote: The police, like the Jesuits, have the one virtue of never abandoning their friends or their enemies

investigator.value: Michu, the huntsman protecting the exiled nobles from police surveillance
investigator.quote: I'd give something, cried Michu, to know how and by whom we have been sold

suspect_architecture.value: Police agents Corentin and Peyrade investigating/pursuing the young exiles and Michu; unclear total count but includes reinstated ci-devants under surveillance
suspect_architecture.quote: we might have seized your ci-devants any day for the last week; but we knew they were reinstated

red_herring.value: The Parisian blacksmith who shoed horses in English fashion was actually a police spy, not just a craftsman
red_herring.quote: So that rascally Parisian blacksmith who shoed the horses in the English fashion...was their spy!

clue_fairness.value: Horse tracks in damp ground revealed by examining horseshoes - clue available to reader when Peyrade explains it
clue_fairness.quote: look at your horses' shoes, and you'll see that you betrayed yourselves

social_world.value: Post-Revolutionary French Empire with reinstated aristocrats (ci-devants) under police surveillance, Bonaparte's regime controlling former exiles
social_world.quote: they were to be under police surveillance for two years, obliged to present themselves at the prefecture

reveal_mechanic.value: Physical evidence of distinctive horseshoe tracks in damp ground leading police to the hiding place
reveal_mechanic.quote: They must have followed our tracks when the ground was damp

_meta.title: ebook honore de balzac an historical mystery
